diary:
Woke up in Woodinville to that early June kind of calm—sun already hinting the day was gonna be decent. I did my quick plant check, grabbed coffee, and headed out before the traffic could get annoying. Mercer Island felt steady and quiet while I worked through tax-season stuff: reconciliations, client spreadsheets, the usual “double-check everything twice” routine. Around late morning I slipped outside for air at Kelley Point Park, just enough to reset my head.

After that, the day picked up. I met up at a downtown bar near Pike Place Market and watched USA vs Wales with everyone leaning into the match like it mattered for the whole week. Then I got back to work, finished my final checks, and handled errands at QFC in Totem Lake.

By evening I biked along Juanita Bay Park—wind, water, and that easy glow. Dinner with a friend at Matt’s in the Market in Greenwood rounded it out. I’m tired, but in a good way—like the city and my own pace finally lined up.
thought_process: I stayed focused at work because tax season always punishes any shortcuts; reconciliations and filing prep are the parts I can’t “wing.” The light rail closure between Westlake and University District made me feel a little boxed in, so I leaned on driving and kept my schedule tight—especially around the 11am USA vs Wales game. I took that short walk at Kelley Point Park because my brain gets stale when I’m staring at numbers too long; fresh air helps me catch mistakes later. Watching the match downtown at Pike Place Market was a deliberate mood shift—something communal to break the monotony. The sunny weather also nudged me to bike Juanita Bay after errands, and by dinner I felt lighter, like I’d earned the downtime instead of just passing through it.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
